required_skills: Real-time graphics programming using DirectX 12, Vulkan, or Metal at a production level
Shader authoring and optimization in HLSL or GLSL for vertex, pixel, compute, and geometry shader stages
Deep understanding of the GPU rendering pipeline including vertex processing, rasterization, fragment shading, and output merging
Physically Based Rendering (PBR) theory and implementation including BRDF models, energy conservation, and material systems
GPU performance profiling and optimization including overdraw reduction, bandwidth minimization, and occupancy improvement
Multi-threaded graphics command buffer recording and synchronization primitives (fences, semaphores, barriers) in modern low-level APIs
Lighting systems development including dynamic shadow mapping, light culling, and global illumination approximations
Cross-platform graphics development and abstraction layer design targeting PC, console, and mobile hardware
preferred_skills: Hardware ray tracing implementation using DirectX Raytracing (DXR) or Vulkan Ray Tracing for reflections, shadows, and GI
Temporal rendering techniques including Temporal Anti-Aliasing (TAA), DLSS 3, FSR 3, and XeSS integration
Mesh shader and amplification shader pipeline development for geometry-heavy scene rendering
Bindless resource architecture design for scalable material and texture management in large open-world games
GPU-driven rendering techniques including indirect draw, GPU culling, and persistent threads
Compute shader development for image processing, particle simulation, and physics on the GPU
Virtual texturing and sparse texture streaming systems for large-scale environment rendering
responsibilities: Design and implement real-time graphics features including lighting models, shadow systems, post-processing effects, and screen-space techniques using DirectX 12 or Vulkan
Author and optimize HLSL and GLSL shaders across all programmable pipeline stages, collaborating with technical artists to deliver high-fidelity, performant visual effects
Develop and maintain the graphics abstraction layer that unifies platform-specific rendering APIs across PC, console, and mobile targets
Profile GPU performance using RenderDoc, Nsight, and platform-specific tools, resolving bottlenecks related to overdraw, memory bandwidth, and shader complexity
Implement physically based rendering systems including material pipelines, image-based lighting (IBL), and BRDF models that support photorealistic art production
Build GPU-driven rendering systems leveraging indirect draw calls, GPU culling pipelines, and bindless resource architectures for large scene scalability
Integrate and validate upscaling technologies such as DLSS, FSR, and XeSS to deliver high visual quality at optimal performance budgets across GPU tiers
Collaborate with engine, gameplay, and VFX teams to expose graphics capabilities through clean, stable APIs and engine integration layers
Research and prototype emerging graphics technologies including hardware ray tracing, mesh shaders, and variable rate shading for potential production integration
Write thorough technical documentation for all graphics systems, ensuring engineering teams have clear references for system behavior, configuration, and extension
